name: -h
--help
options.description: Show help message and exit

name: -V
--version
options.description: Show the version and exit

name: -h
--help
options.description: help for build

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Silence warnings

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Enable verbose output

name: -f
--config-file
options.description: Provide a specific MkDocs config

name: -s
--strict
options.description: Enable strict mode. This will cause MkDocs to abort the build on any warnings

name: -t
--theme
options.description: The theme to use when building your documentation

options.name: --use-directory-urls
options.description: Use directory URLs when building pages (the default)

options.name: --no-directory-urls
options.description: Don't use directory URLs when building pages

name: -c
--clean
options.description: Remove old files from the site directory before building (the default)

options.name: --dirty
options.description: Only rebuild pages that have been modified since last build

name: -d
--site-dir
options.description: The directory to output the result of the documentation build

subcommands.name: gh-deploy
subcommands.description: Deploy your documentation to GitHub Pages

name: -h
--help
options.description: help for gh-deploy

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Silence warnings

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Enable verbose output

name: -f
--config-file
options.description: Provide a specific MkDocs config

name: -s
--strict
options.description: Enable strict mode. This will cause MkDocs to abort the build on any warnings

name: -t
--theme
options.description: The theme to use when building your documentation

options.name: --use-directory-urls
options.description: Use directory URLs when building pages (the default)

options.name: --no-directory-urls
options.description: Don't use directory URLs when building pages

name: -c
--clean
options.description: Remove old files from the site directory before building (the default)

options.name: --dirty
options.description: Only rebuild pages that have been modified since last build

name: -m
--message
options.description: A commit message to use when committing to the GitHub Pages remote branch. Commit {sha} and MkDocs {version} are available as expansions

name: -b
--remote-branch
options.description: The remote branch to commit to for GitHub Pages. This overrides the value specified in config

name: -r
--remote-name
options.description: The remote name to commit to for GitHub Pages. This overrides the value specified in config

options.name: --force
options.description: Force the push to the repository

options.name: --no-history
options.description: Replace the whole Git history with one new commit

options.name: --ignore-version
options.description: Ignore check that build is not being deployed with an older version of MkDocs

options.name: --shell
options.description: Use the shell when invoking Git

name: -h
--help
options.description: help for new

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Silence warnings

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Enable verbose output

name: -h
--help
options.description: help for serve

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Silence warnings

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Enable verbose output

name: -f
--config-file
options.description: Provide a specific MkDocs config

name: -s
--strict
options.description: Enable strict mode. This will cause MkDocs to abort the build on any warnings

name: -t
--theme
options.description: The theme to use when building your documentation

options.name: --use-directory-urls
options.description: Use directory URLs when building pages (the default)

options.name: --no-directory-urls
options.description: Don't use directory URLs when building pages

name: -a
--dev-addr
options.description: IP address and port to serve documentation locally (default: localhost:8000)

options.name: --live-reload
options.description: Enable the live reloading in the development server (this is the default)

options.name: --no-reload
options.description: Disable the live reloading in the development server

options.name: --dirtyreload
options.description: Enable the live reloading in the development server, but only re-build files that have changed

options.name: --watch-theme
options.description: Include the theme in list of files to watch for live reloading. Ignored when live reload is not used

name: -w
--watch
options.description: A directory or file to watch for live reloading. Can be supplied multiple times
